---
title: "Solar Cooker IoT Testing Platform"
description: "MSc thesis - open-source, sub-$100 IoT data acquisition unit replacing $10K+ lab equipment for ASAE S580.1 solar cooker performance testing."
format: html
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="case-study-header" data-aos="fade-up">
  <span class="role-badge">MSc Research &bull; University of Nairobi</span>
  <h1>Solar Cooker IoT Testing Platform</h1>
  <p>A low-cost, open-source framework for linking standardized performance to the multidimensional impacts of solar cookers - replacing $10,000+ lab equipment with a sub-$100 IoT system.</p>
  <span class="private-badge" style="margin-top: 1rem; font-size: 0.85rem;">&#128274; Private Repository</span>
</div>
```

::: {.case-study-section data-aos="fade-up"}
## Problem

Solar cookers can significantly reduce fuel use, indoor air pollution, and CO&#8322; emissions in developing regions. But **validating their performance** requires expensive lab-grade instrumentation that most organizations can't afford.

The ASAE S580.1 standard defines how to measure cooking power, but no affordable, field-ready testing system existed. This creates a **data vacuum** - innovators and institutions in the Global South cannot generate the verified performance evidence needed for results-based climate financing.

**The cost barrier**: Reference-grade pyranometers and data loggers cost upwards of $10,000. My target: **under $100**.
:::

::: {.case-study-section data-aos="fade-up"}
## Solution

I designed a complete testing system spanning hardware, simulation, and software:

**Hardware - ESP32-Based Sensor Node**

- ESP32 microcontroller with MicroPython firmware
- BPW34 silicon PIN photodiode with PTFE cosine corrector (irradiance measurement)
- MCP6022 dual op-amp transimpedance amplifier (signal conditioning)
- PT100 RTD + MAX31865 (water temperature)
- DHT22 (ambient temperature/humidity)
- Anemometer (wind speed)
- SIM808 GSM module (telemetry), MicroSD (local storage)
- **Custom PCB designed in KiCad** - BOM total ~$1.58 for the analog front-end

**Simulation & Validation Pipeline (Python)**

- SciPy - automated analog component optimization (E-series resistor snapping)
- PySpice/Ngspice - multi-domain circuit simulation (DC, AC, transient, noise analysis)
- **9-stage CI/CD hardware pipeline**: analytical design → E-series snap → circuit synthesis → SPICE simulation → KiCad PCB export → PCB layout (FreeRouting) → DRC/Gerber export → firmware generation → calibration validation
- Feedback loop: re-optimizes analog design if SPICE results diverge from analytical predictions

**Software - R Backend + Dashboard**

- R Plumber REST API for data ingestion, querying, device management, test sessions, and ASAE S580.1 analysis
- SQLite database with migration-based schema
- Golem-structured Shiny dashboard for real-time visualization
- Automatic standardization of cooking power to 700 W/m² per ASAE S580.1
- Sustainability impact calculators: fuel savings (kg/year), CO&#8322; avoided (tonnes), health benefit proxies
:::

::: {.case-study-section data-aos="fade-up"}
## Results

::: {.result-card}
**Delivered a field-ready testing system at a fraction of commercial equipment cost** - enabling standardized solar cooker evaluation where it was previously impossible.
:::

**Design Specifications:**

- **Cost**: Sub-$100 (vs. $10,000+ for gold standard)
- **ADC**: ESP32 12-bit, ENOB validated
- **Bandwidth**: ≥ 100 kHz target
- **Phase margin**: ≥ 60 degrees
- **Noise floor**: < 1 ADC LSB (0.8 mV)
- **Linearity error**: < 0.5%
- **Calibration**: ISO 9847 + ASAE S580.1

**Impact:**

- Compliant with the **ASAE S580.1** international standard
- Testing conducted at UoN's SCI-accredited Regional Testing and Knowledge Centre
- Open-source design allows replication by researchers and NGOs
- 6 GitHub Actions CI workflows: app, Docker, firmware, hardware, simulations, thesis
:::

::: {.case-study-section data-aos="fade-up"}
## Tech Stack

```{=html}
<div class="skill-tags" style="margin-top: 0.5rem;">
  <span class="skill-tag primary">ESP32</span>
  <span class="skill-tag primary">MicroPython</span>
  <span class="skill-tag primary">BPW34 Photodiode</span>
  <span class="skill-tag primary">PT100 RTD</span>
  <span class="skill-tag green">Python</span>
  <span class="skill-tag green">SciPy</span>
  <span class="skill-tag green">PySpice / Ngspice</span>
  <span class="skill-tag green">KiCad</span>
  <span class="skill-tag accent">R Plumber API</span>
  <span class="skill-tag accent">Golem / Shiny</span>
  <span class="skill-tag orange">Docker Compose</span>
  <span class="skill-tag orange">GitHub Actions CI</span>
  <span class="skill-tag orange">Quarto</span>
</div>
```
:::

```{=html}
<div class="contact-section" data-aos="fade-up">
  <h2>Interested in collaborating or deploying this system?</h2>
  <p>I welcome researchers, NGOs, and developers to contribute or adapt this platform for field use.</p>
  <div class="contact-links">
    <a href="mailto:robert.kimutai.ds@gmail.com" class="contact-link email">Contact Me</a>
    <a href="index.html" class="contact-link linkedin">Back to Portfolio</a>
  </div>
</div>
```
